name: floor_penetration_base_pinning

symptoms: Mobile base NAV commands have zero effect — base joints stay at start position despite position targets
Base appears frozen or pinned even when joint controllers are active
trace_evidence: Wheel<->floor contact impulses ~10,000 N·s (vs ~7 N·s expected from robot weight alone)
Wheel mesh collision geometry extends 7.3 mm below z=0 while floor top surface is at z=0
Constant floor interpenetration generates enormous normal forces that pin the base
Fixed/virtual root robot: wheel meshes loaded below floor plane at default spawn height

when_to_apply: Any time a wheeled or skid-steer mobile base spawns at z=0 and NAV commands produce no movement, especially when contact force logs show abnormally high normal impulses on wheel links.
repair_strategy: Measure the lowest collision point of the base (mesh z-bounds + cumulative joint origins from root to wheel bottom). Spawn the robot root high enough that wheel bottoms REST on z=0 surface (we used +9 mm offset). With a fixed/virtual root, the robot weight is carried by the root joint so resting wheels have near-zero normal force and the base slides freely under NAV commands.
code_sketch: # Measure lowest wheel point (run once at asset load time)
# Verify fix: contact impulse on wheel links should drop to ~7 N·s
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro (ManiSkill GPU sim) 2026-07-01: root_x command 0.10 -> reached 0.10 exactly after +9 mm spawn offset; NAV then hit stations to mm accuracy
